definition: Compute the spatial, spatiotemporal, vertical, stream-network, or other task-specific prediction distance from each evaluation location to the training set. This is a SPECTRA-style audit for whether error increases as the intended prediction horizon moves away from observed training support.

citations.title: Spatial machine-learning model diagnostics: a model-agnostic distance-based approach

evidence.summary: The paper proposes prediction-distance profiles to assess spatial machine learning models.
Spatial leave-one-out records separation distance and performance as a function of distance.
The paper argues model assessment should relate performance to intended spatial prediction distance.

limitations: Euclidean or haversine distance may be wrong for streams, roads, vertical geology, or anisotropic processes.
Spatial distance can be confounded with environmental covariates and sampling density.
Interpolation and transfer are different scientific objectives and should not be collapsed.
quality_gates: Report the distance metric and why it matches the prediction horizon.
Compare prediction-distance curves to environmental-feature and domain-metadata curves.
Report train/evaluation sampling density and nearest-neighbor distance distributions.
